speaker: interviewee
text: Now, excuse me. Who is going to see the video? I told you about--

speaker: interviewer
text: This is tape number five.

speaker: interviewee
text: --my sister.

speaker: interviewer
text: The survivor is Mr. Victor Dortheimer. Before we took a tape break, you-- you were talking about your experiences at Gross-Rosen, you explaining what they were like. How long were you there for?

speaker: interviewee
text: Four days.

speaker: interviewer
text: And did you have a uniform? Were you--

speaker: interviewee
text: Stripes. Stripes.

speaker: interviewer
text: And were you given a number?

speaker: interviewee
text: We got a number. We got a hat, a special hat-- green. And we have pullover, and jacket, stripes, and trousers, stripes, and shoes, but no underwear. We get just-- I don't know what was it, you know?

speaker: interviewee
text: And no socks. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] OK. So we went to Schindler. I was unconscious two times [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] during the-- the-- the transport. Because when [? it ?] 6 o'clock, they put us into wagons. And everyone got three slices of bread. And from-- from hunger I was two times unconscious, OK?

speaker: interviewee
text: Eventually, we came to the factory. Schindler was waiting. [INAUDIBLE] Schindler, director. So we went to factory. Was two miles. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] No beds, nothing. Just straw-- straw. No beds, nothing. I was laying on the straw hungry first day. And the-- my friend went out, you know. They're working. They got a piece of bread. They got a potato. And I'm hungry. Next day in the morning, come on. I think Schindler wanted to see you.

speaker: interviewee
text: Where are you? I went down because [INAUDIBLE] floor in fact. Excuse me, what are you doing? You should organize your job, OK? I said, director, I'm hungry. OK, come with me. It was like, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] oh, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] if you-- no, I-- a room with food. In the-- in the corner was bread. Take it and get-- get well. I took a bread and potato, and I start to work, OK?

speaker: interviewee
text: And I said, OK, director. I'm going to organize. Take people because we were too many people. You must-- they give the work. [INAUDIBLE] don't worry.

speaker: interviewee
text: So we start to work. What was to work? Nothing-- nothing to work. But suddenly, the SS, they wanted to paint the rooms for them. And so I start to be busy. And I took another three or four people, you know? I said, it was old man [? Ferber. ?]

speaker: interviewee
text: I show you-- very old. [? Maybe 70. ?] How he came to us, I don't know. I says, [? Ferber, ?] sit here. You got brush. You got-- when somebody's coming, paint. And this was the joke. But I was free. And I was always [INAUDIBLE]. The commander wanted to make friend. [INAUDIBLE] [? a proper ?] commander, [DRINKS] [INAUDIBLE]. This other guy, what, he killed thousands and thousands of Jews in Poland. And he was our commander.

speaker: interviewee
text: We've been in the camp. And in the camp, he got two rooms with kitchen, everything. And he took one girl to cook, you know? [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] It was a murder. And he suddenly-- where's the painter? I need a painter because it's dirty. So I went to him. He said, painter, you're going to do the-- all right, sure. I said, boss, but I need flour. You know this flour? What? Flour in the war? Are you crazy?

speaker: interviewee
text: I said, excuse me. I need a glue. But glue is not here. I-- I need flour. And he said, OK. If you need flour, you are clever and go to the kitchen to the Jewish-- and tell them that you need flour. You know. Bastard.

speaker: interviewee
text: So I went [? to ?] the [? boss ?] kitchen. It was German Jews. I said, listen. I need five kilo flour. I didn't need nothing because I want flour to make, you know? To bake something. I said, [INAUDIBLE]. Oh, no, no, not here. OK. I'm going to Leipold.

speaker: interviewee
text: Don't go to Leipold, please. [LAUGHS] This was my job. And after, I have Polish friends. And I was [? smuggling. ?] I can't tell you. I was like a king in his home.

speaker: interviewee
text: Bread, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. You know the [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]? [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], you know this [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]? No. From pig, the fat. And so the Polish give me, my friends. I-- I met my friends [INAUDIBLE] the same school, the same class as me. We were [INAUDIBLE]. And then Schindler? Oh, a big smoker-- I was smoking every day. I took a box of cigarettes, take a one. I left, I take one. How big-- was the factory larger or smaller compared to the Emalia factory?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was-- we have a production of Emalia-- Emalia, just arms. Should be arm, but we didn't produce it. But also a little bit smaller. This factory was textile. Once was Jewish-- Jewish. But the Jewish, they went to England. The German took over. But Herr Schindler, I don't know how he managed to take over again. I don't know. I don't know.

speaker: interviewer
text: But what was the size like? Was it smaller than Emalia.

speaker: interviewee
text: Generally was-- was smaller, yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: And how long were you there for?

speaker: interviewee
text: We?

speaker: interviewer
text: Yeah.

speaker: interviewee
text: From October until May-- eighth of May.

speaker: interviewer
text: Did you-- what type of job did you do there again?

speaker: interviewee
text: What I was doing?

speaker: interviewer
text: Yeah.

speaker: interviewee
text: Painting-- painting and smuggling.

speaker: interviewer
text: And did you-- were-- was-- was Brunnlitz ever inspected by other SS officers?

speaker: interviewee
text: Me? No, never, never. They have nothing to do with SS. They were local in the factory.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what about the women? What did the women do?

speaker: interviewee
text: Nothing. They-- they got-- before, it was textile fabric factory. And they got a rule. And they-- they made pullover underwear. And what a thing to do!

speaker: interviewer
text: And the sleeping arrangements?

speaker: interviewee
text: Sleeping is also OK. It was warm, central heating. Beds-- two in one bed.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the washing facilities?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was OK. When they came, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] it was-- was sleeping on the straw. In three, four weeks, everybody got lice. But which-- which kind of lice?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was terrible. One day, they didn't let us in because we were in factory, you know? On the-- rooms-- rooms-- rooms, big rooms, they didn't let us in.

speaker: interviewee
text: They took every clothes from us, groups. They put in high [INAUDIBLE] oven-- steam. And they-- they damaged every lice, everything that was possible. And they went shower for us. Hair, everything, up-- and it was everything. We come upstairs-- new beds, new blankets, new mattress, everything new. This was good. Now, the privilege every day was a shower. The privilege was maybe 10 people to get-- I was [INAUDIBLE] have a beautiful shower. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] So it was doctors, and we have never saw lice anymore.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is there anything else that you'd like to say about the-- the factory in Czechoslovakia, Schindler's factory?

speaker: interviewee
text: Downstairs, I had my workshop. This was [? red ?] [? pine ?] and green powder. Between the powder, I had tobacco, a piece of bread. I was during-- working during the day. And in the night, [INAUDIBLE] other people. When I came in the morning, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] somebody told me, you know, [INAUDIBLE] he want [INAUDIBLE]. He was looking in new cases about this. And I-- I saw and they steal me some. And I knew who is the [INAUDIBLE] one. And somebody told me about [INAUDIBLE]. And I-- I [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewee
text: So I hit him. I beat him, honest. And not because he [? steal ?] me-- the people told me he came from Tarnow, what is another town. Did you hear about Tarnow? And he was after the Jews, gone. They were looking for Jews where they're hiding.

speaker: interviewee
text: And they shot them, everyone. He was agent for gestapo. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] And I hit him. And director-- I said, director-- director listen. [? This is ?] [? an agent. ?]

speaker: interviewee
text: This was [INAUDIBLE], you know? And you know what happened to the [INAUDIBLE]? After the war, he went to Krakow, and they arrest him, the Polish, and they hang him. [INAUDIBLE]. Do you remember [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] celebrating anybody's birthday when you were in the factories?

speaker: interviewee
text: Schindler-- Schindler's birthday. [INAUDIBLE] say I don't remember which one. But we get cakes. You know, like [? holidays. ?] Everyone makes [? half ?] bread [INAUDIBLE]. This was a celebration.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where did they get the cakes from?

speaker: interviewee
text: Ask him.

speaker: interviewer
text: He brought it in?

speaker: interviewee
text: Of course he brought it. [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewer
text: And keeping any festivals? Did anybody manage to keep any festivals there? Or Shabbat?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, no, no, no Shabbat-- nothing. It was too much. I remember the women. This was factory for the arms. Outside, the SS would shoot-- this is the-- the-- the duty was looking that nobody was going to run away. But inside, they had nothing-- as I said, nothing to say. Our women was 300. They [INAUDIBLE] to do, because it was cold, and they [INAUDIBLE] to pee, you know, very often. And they couldn't go out for themselves. One SS woman should take them out.

speaker: interviewee
text: But they were [? bastard. ?] They didn't want, you know? And this was one officer from Wehrmacht what is there from the arms production. And he said, what is going on? He was no Nazi. In [? Provokativ, ?] the-- the-- maybe 20 women wanted to go to [INAUDIBLE]. And the-- the SS refuse the women.

speaker: interviewee
text: And he took the two women, come with me. And he went to the-- there was big trouble, but he said, ah, they must producing arms [INAUDIBLE]. It was a fight between SS and Wehrmacht.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where were you when you were liberated?

speaker: interviewee
text: [DRINKS] In factory.

speaker: interviewer
text: And do you remember that day?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, 8 of May. It was the end of the war. And in the morning, 8 May, director said, 3 o'clock, I'm going to put radio. And everybody's going to listen to radio. It was [? pick of Churchill, ?] unconditional capitulation. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] And Schindler had a speech.

speaker: interviewee
text: And they told us, we know what happened. They killed innocent people, millions of them. But please, not everyone is guilty. And please, don't take any, you know, how-- how you say-- get back at them, you know? Revenge.

speaker: interviewee
text: Let them through the courts and behave you like human being. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] And when-- when he was speaking, I was jumping [? on him ?] and kissing him, and--

speaker: interviewer
text: And where were the guards that had protected you?

speaker: interviewee
text: The guards-- just one day before, they went. They came-- there was one SS man with us. He stayed, but he was a Pollack. They took him, you know? When the Russians came, immediately, what are you doing? And witnesses. And they proved this is OK, [INAUDIBLE] let them go. But there was a group maybe for 12 people. They came to us two months before the war finished. [DRINKS]

speaker: interviewee
text: This was 15 Jews and one German. And the German was all prisoner in concentration camp because he was professional murder. The German put him in. And these people, these 15 people, told us they went kilometers, kilometer. And this German killed a lot of Jewish prisoner because for bread, you know? He was helping the SS.

speaker: interviewee
text: OK, we knew that. When they were finished, this prisoner, what was his-- they cut him. And the Russian came. And they wanted. And the Russian commander, I don't know, high ranking, report with the SS there, where he killed, how he killed. And he said, OK, do what you-- what you want.

speaker: interviewee
text: OK, so they took him. How-- I don't know how. Immediately, they got revolvers. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And it was a big machine. And they took him to the machine, make a-- you know, like-- had to hang him. No, how you say? A noose.

speaker: interviewee
text: Stick.

speaker: interviewee
text: A noose.

speaker: interviewee
text: They put him. And he [INAUDIBLE]. I said, ah, OK. I'm unlucky. I was-- [LAUGHS] they hanged him, they fell down. Because-- [INAUDIBLE] shot-- shot him. Another was [INAUDIBLE] terrible. It was terrible.

speaker: interviewee
text: They killed him [INAUDIBLE] like blood animal. And well, [INAUDIBLE] the revolver. There was a [INAUDIBLE] revolver. [INAUDIBLE] And the Russian came, what are you doing? He gave him two in the face. Give me that revolver, you bastard, [? you! ?] They behave like animal. You know? I'm going to tell you, the Jews is [? coming ?] Russian officer's women with [INAUDIBLE]. Come with me to the Jews. We're going to rope the German. They went to German village, [INAUDIBLE] German. You bloody bastard [INAUDIBLE]. Where your-- where's husband? I don't know. You are murderer. These Russians hit them. Take what you want! And [INAUDIBLE] [? used up, ?] not me. OK. Do you remember when Schindler left the factory?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, yeah, of course. It was 8 of May, after his speech. He told me, come with me because I'm going. I want to go away because the Russian coming. And I haven't got a driver. My future brother-in-law was a driver. I said, [NON-ENGLISH]. He died now. Go with Schindler. Please go. I [? wanted ?] my wife. Please, I want her to. No, she's afraid. His sister, her sister [INAUDIBLE] his future, she said, OK. You're a coward. OK, I'm going with [? Richard. ?] And I organized. I said, director is going to [? OK. ?] And he took her revolver. Please, look after me before I'm going. Because I don't trust [? them. ?]

speaker: interviewee
text: So I [? got the ?] revolver. But be careful, because if Marta is here, [INAUDIBLE]. This is my wife. Tell me that [INAUDIBLE]. Don't want it. And this was the final. She gave me the picture because I wanted something. We'll come onto that when we look at the pictures.

speaker: interviewee
text: And the [INAUDIBLE] he went, OK. And I'm regretting that didn't [INAUDIBLE] see here again and again. I was in German, he was in Israel [INAUDIBLE]. But he-- he died 64. He was 64 years old.

speaker: interviewer
text: Did you not keep in contact with him?

speaker: interviewee
text: How was possible? My wife [INAUDIBLE], my son, my son. You are the son of the [? martyr, ?] of the painter. Yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: And do you remember him being given a ring? Like in the film they show that he's been given a ring by the workers of the factory.

speaker: interviewee
text: No, I don't know nothing about.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when he was taken-- when he was driven away by Richard, what was he wearing? What clothes was him-- him and Emily wearing?

speaker: interviewee
text: Normal-- normal clothes with his wife, with 15 Jews together. What happened after I know from my sister in [INAUDIBLE]. When they went, they were Russian already. They give him the hat and [INAUDIBLE] something like that [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: And what were his last words to you?

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE] nothing. Nothing. I was crying. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] Yeah. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] Not [INAUDIBLE] last word, the situation-- I wanted help him to rescue him. He was looking how-- somehow to get out. Because he knows that the Russian is coming. And then the Russian going to cut him, is going to hang him. Because he was a spy. What [? happened ?] to [? Waldenburg? ?] They're animals.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was he intending to go when he was being driven off?

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE]?

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was he intending to go when he was being driven off?

speaker: interviewee
text: Where he wanted--

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was he going?

speaker: interviewee
text: To American [? soil. ?] He know there's American there, yeah. But he-- he went to the border of Switzerland, yeah, towards Germany.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who was with them?

speaker: interviewee
text: Jewish people, Jewish people-- always Jewish people, yeah. He was with the Jews. After this is another story. After the war, he went to Argentine. He joined and this is another story. This is not my story.

speaker: interviewer
text: But who went in the car with him when they were being driven off?

speaker: interviewee
text: Hoyberger, Eric. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Howe we say-- another. I know-- I-- I knew the names-- Pinkus, Esther; Richard Drecken. Who was-- Dora, Dora, Dora, [INAUDIBLE] I forgotted the name. A few names [? I got. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: So after you were liberated, where did you go from there?

speaker: interviewee
text: When the Russian came, I had my friend with his wife. We took a small house, and we were-- food, the check they give us, food. And they prepare a train to Krakow, [INAUDIBLE] beautiful for everyone, bed and food. So we went to Krakow.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what did you do when you were in Krakow?

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE]. I took my my-- my-- you know-- my [INAUDIBLE]. My [INAUDIBLE] 10 kilo potatoes. [INAUDIBLE] Czechoslovakia and some clothes what Schindler gave us when he left for everyone, you know? For coats, for-- when they came to Krakow, I said, [? Hela-- ?] she is from Krakow. I am from Krakow-- you stay here in the station.

speaker: interviewee
text: I'm going to my flat. I wanted to see what-- I walked, I walked, I walked. No hat, no coat. Police patrol, police solider and Russian-- stop. Where are you? [INAUDIBLE]? I said, I'm from concentration camp. They spoke Polish. Oh, you are from [INAUDIBLE], all right. OK, go. I went. It was small market, square. And-- this was [? Droska. ?] You know what is? A-- a horse without [? that. ?] It's a taxi. I go, excuse me.

speaker: interviewee
text: I came from concentration-- in Polish. My wife in-- in the station. I haven't got money. If you want, take me there and take me there. Maybe I'm going to find some friend who is going to pay you. OK. So he went. He took us.

speaker: interviewee
text: We came to our place where I was living from before. Outside is my friend, Polish. I said, [INAUDIBLE], first, pay him. OK, he paid him. He said, who is here? This is some Jew.

speaker: interviewee
text: Your cousin is here. OK, so my cousin with our friend got one room with a kitchen. So we went for two weeks and we went to Czechoslovakia again.

speaker: interviewer
text: Did you ever return to your house in Krakow?

speaker: interviewee
text: Somebody was living there. I went to my-- and this was a widow with her children. But the husband was killed for the-- the German killed him. You know. And the same was in my wife's place.
